digest.component_or_area: title validation in composer commands (CLI/library/MCP facades)
propositions: Whitespace-only titles are wrongly accepted because validation uses `if not title:`, which only catches the empty string.
Titles must be rejected iff `title.strip()` is empty, consistently across CLI, library, and MCP create and edit surfaces.
A title with surrounding whitespace and real content must be accepted and stored byte-identically without silent trimming.
Unicode values must round-trip byte-identically per-form (NFC and NFD treated as distinct), except the pre-existing documented `→`→`->` substitution.
Create-path tests must assert rejection of empty and >255-char titles, and NUL/argv-impossible values covered via library/MCP tests only.
This is a user-visible tightening requiring a back-out note, a pre-merge store scan for existing whitespace titles, and updated CHANGELOG and CLI help docs.
